programme.desc: In December 1981, a New Jersey state trooper was shot to death on the side of Interstate 80. Fingerprints on the suspect's vehicle pointed to a fugitive wanted on bank robbery charges.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and several spurned lovers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, a California man's body was found dismembered. Six months later, the murder suspect was caught.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The First and Kennedy Street Crew
programme.desc: Powerful street gangs in Washington D.C. enforced their power with deadly violence. The gang leader had fled to Africa, a hunt that ended in the 1994 Washington D.C. Metropolitan Police Headquarters shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI discovered that the criminals had a hidden agenda beyond their personal wealth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February 1988, a record store clerk in Ohio was fatally shot in his van. After discovering thousands of dollars in the vehicle, police knew that robbery was not the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A drug dealer engaged in a shootout with Nebraska state troopers and evaded capture after a high-speed chase.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the 23-year-old killer who murdered 17-year-old former head girl Georgia Williams in 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the most feared men in US prison history, Thomas Silverstein became a gang leader for the Aryan Brotherhood on the inside. After savagely killing multiple inmates and a correctional officer, he spent 36 years in solitary confinement.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Willing to kill for just $20 and drugs, Joshua Gardner commits his first murder at 24. Even behind bars, he can't contain his rage, brutally beating a fellow inmate to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After cold-blooded killer Leo Boatman murdered two campers, he went on the kill three inmates behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Having committed over 27 assaults on correctional officers, Billy Tracy is one of the most violent offenders on death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A master manipulator accused of the murder of his wife eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancée.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Colin Pitchfork is a child murder who killed fifteen-year-olds Lynda Mann and Dawn Ashworth in the 1980s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Goudeau terrorized victims in the Phoenix metro area between August 2005 and June 2006. In addition to committing nine murders, his extensive crime spree included 84 other felony crimes, totaling 93 felonies over the ten-month period.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile into the murderer who terrorised the gay community as the 'Trash Bag Killer' and confessed to killing 32 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In April 1997, 27-year-old Rachel McGrath was stabbed to death by an unknown attacker in a car park. 24 hours later and 100 miles away, a terrified 17-year-old student flagged down a motorist, telling them she'd just escaped from a man.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Keith Jesperson, known as the Smiley Face Killer, killed eight women across several states in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Douglas Clark and Carol Bundy were a deadly duo who terrorised the streets of LA in the summer of 1980.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A master manipulator accused of the murder of his wife eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancée.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Westley developed predatory urges as a teen, escalating his crimes over the years. By 28, he murdered three young boys before a failed attempt to take another child exposed him and ended his deadly spree.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Young Natalie Jarvis was killed by Adam Whelehan, whose cruel and cowardly plot was exposed by investigators.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who attacked his ex-girlfriend in a jealous frenzy and committed a brutal murder.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After cold-blooded killer Leo Boatman murdered two campers, he went on the kill three inmates behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, 22-year-old Dawn Bruce is brutally murdered in her Virginia apartment. Investigators notice a blood smear on a pillowcase that appears to have been made by one of the killer's fingers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, six-year-old Cassie Hansen disappeared from her St. Paul, Minnesota church during an evening service.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Professional photographer Charles Rathbun claims model Linda Sobek died during a consensual sexual encounter gone wrong, but Sobek's corpse and some high-tech digital imagery tell a more sinister story.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1998, an evening out at a Maryland murder mystery theatre performance turns into a real-life whodunit when the badly burned body of Stephen Hricko is discovered in his hotel room after a fire.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1980, Michigan resident Shannon Mohr died in what was reported to be a horseback riding accident by her new husband David Davis. After her death, Shannon's family reported to police their suspicions that Davis seemed far from grief-stricken.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a human skull retrieved from an Ohio pond reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate that the victim had been stabbed multiple times and that the teeth had been removed with needle-nose pliers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In rural Canada, Dr. John Schneeberger is accused of sedating and sexually assaulting one of his female patients and his stepdaughter. DNA tests demonstrate that the doctor is innocent, but the patient insists that he sedated and raped her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a search for the killer of two teenagers in Texas, a behavioral profile led to suspect Jason Massey, and hard science proved the profile was correct. Massey was executed by lethal injection on April 3, 2001.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronologist conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Susie Mowbray was charged for her husband Bill Mowbray's death, but her son was convinced of her innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, San Francisco pornographer Artie Mitchell was murdered in his home. His brother and business partner Jim is found near the scene carrying a rifle and later confesses to shooting Artie. The question is whether the shooting was premeditated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, New York correctional officer Donna Payant disappeared and was later found in a landfill. The medical examiner not only identified the cause of death but also found an important clue.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Lori Auker left her Pennsylvania home for work and never arrived, it took space-age technology, cat hairs and insects to pinpoint the image of the woman's abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fan of murder mysteries and forensic science shows uses what she learned from TV to determine who was responsible for the accident which permanently injured her 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping but didn't return home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two boys are brutally attacked, investigators rely on evidence fished out of a nearby pond. It became the first case where diatom analysis was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6-year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know - without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanished after attending a party. The prime suspect had two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome circumstantial evidence, but forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When more than 700 Oregonians contracted food poisoning from a unique strain of salmonella in 1984, investigators uncovered a secret plot to influence local elections.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened, and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered among garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.
